rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in the component type scope
Place implementation contracts in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers when referring to ports or other features in contracts
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic in AGREE contracts
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure when inserting annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for component type contracts
Use valid implementation scopes for implementation contracts
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic in AGREE contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not(...), and implication =>
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic in AGREE contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Subcomponent ports should not be used in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
For component type contracts, place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees inside annex agree only when the case supplies concrete bounds, named constants, or a visible relation
For output dependency contracts, write a guarantee that relates visible outputs to visible inputs
If AGREE reports 'The assumed type ... is ... but the actual type is ...', align the helper declaration type with the expression

If diagnostics report unresolved references to Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, Period, Priority, Dispatch_Protocol, Stack_Size, Code_Size, or unit tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes inside an AGREE annex, delete the generated clause
